metadata.summary: Quantinuum's common representation for quantum programs

documentation:
hugr
===============

[![build_status][]](https://github.com/CQCL/hugr/actions)
[![codecov][]](https://codecov.io/gh/CQCL/hugr)

The Hierarchical Unified Graph Representation (HUGR, pronounced _hugger_) is the
common representation of quantum circuits and operations in the Quantinuum
ecosystem.

This library provides a pure-python implementation of the HUGR data model, and
a low-level API for constructing HUGR objects.

The API documentation for this package is [here](https://cqcl.github.io/hugr/).

This library is intended to be used as a dependency for other high-level tools.
See [`guppylang`][] and [`tket2`][] for examples of such tools.

The HUGR specification is [here](https://github.com/CQCL/hugr/blob/main/specification/hugr.md).

  [`guppylang`]: https://pypi.org/project/guppylang/
  [`tket2`]: https://github.com/CQCL/tket2


## Installation

The package name is `hugr`. It can be installed from PyPI:
```bash
pip install hugr
```

The current releases are in alpha stage, and the API is subject to change.

classes.name: hugr.utils.BiMap

methods.name: hugr.utils.BiMap.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a bidirectional map.

Args:
    fwd: Left to right mapping. Defaults to empty.

Raises:
    NotBijection: If the initial map is not a bijection.

methods.name: hugr.utils.BiMap.__getitem__
documentation:
Get the right value for a left key.

Args:
    key: Left key.

Raises:
    KeyError: If the key is not found.

Example:
    >>> bm = BiMap({"a": 1})
    >>> bm["a"]
    1

methods.name: hugr.utils.BiMap.__setitem__
documentation: See {meth}`insert_left`.

methods.name: hugr.utils.BiMap.__delitem__
documentation: See {meth}`delete_left`.

methods.name: hugr.utils.BiMap.items
documentation:
Iterator over left, right pairs.

Example:
    >>> bm = BiMap({"a": 1, "b": 2})
    >>> list(bm.items())
    [('a', 1), ('b', 2)]

methods.name: hugr.utils.BiMap.get_left
documentation:
Get a left value using a right key.

Example:
    >>> bm = BiMap({"a": 1})
    >>> bm.get_left(1)
    'a'
    >>> bm.get_left(2)

methods.name: hugr.utils.BiMap.get_right
documentation:
Get a right value using a left key.

Example:
    >>> bm = BiMap({"a": 1})
    >>> bm.get_right("a")
    1
    >>> bm.get_right("b")

methods.name: hugr.utils.BiMap.insert_left
documentation:
Insert a left key and right value.
If the key or value already exist, the existing key-value pair is replaced.

Args:
    key: Left key.
    value: Right value.

Example:
    >>> bm = BiMap()
    >>> bm.insert_left("a", 1)
    >>> bm["a"]
    1

methods.name: hugr.utils.BiMap.insert_right
documentation:
Insert a right key and left value.
If the key or value already exist, the existing key-value pair is replaced.

Args:
    key: Right key.
    value: Left value.

Example:
    >>> bm = BiMap()
    >>> bm.insert_right(1, "a")
    >>> bm["a"]
    1

methods.name: hugr.utils.BiMap.delete_left
documentation:
Delete a left key and its right value.

Args:
    key: Left key.

Raises:
    KeyError: If the key is not found.

Example:
    >>> bm = BiMap({"a": 1})
    >>> bm.delete_left("a")
    >>> bm
    BiMap({})

methods.name: hugr.utils.BiMap.delete_right
documentation:
Delete a right key and its left value.

Args:
    key: Right key.

Raises:
    KeyError: If the key is not found.

Example:
    >>> bm = BiMap({"a": 1})
    >>> bm.delete_right(1)
    >>> bm
    BiMap({})

Bidirectional map backed by two dictionaries, between left types `L` and
right types `R`.

classes.name: hugr.hugr.base.Hugr

methods.name: hugr.hugr.base.Hugr.nodes
documentation: Iterator over nodes of the hugr and their data.

methods.name: hugr.hugr.base.Hugr.links
documentation:
Iterator over all the links in the HUGR.

Returns:
    Iterator of pairs of outgoing port and the incoming ports.

methods.name: hugr.hugr.base.Hugr.children
documentation:
The child nodes of a given `node`.

Args:
    node: Parent node. Defaults to the HUGR root.

Returns:
    List of child nodes.

Examples:
    >>> h = Hugr()
    >>> n = h.add_node(ops.Const(val.TRUE))
    >>> h.children(h.root)
    [Node(1)]

methods.name: hugr.hugr.base.Hugr._update_node_outs
documentation:
Update the number of outgoing ports for a node.

Returns:
    The updated node.

methods.name: hugr.hugr.base.Hugr.add_node
documentation:
Add a node to the HUGR.

Args:
    op: Operation of the node.
    parent: Parent node of added node. Defaults to HUGR root if None.
    num_outs: Number of output ports expected for this node. Defaults to None.
    metadata: A dictionary of metadata to associate with the node.
        Defaults to None.

Returns:
    Handle to the added node.

methods.name: hugr.hugr.base.Hugr.add_const
documentation:
Add a constant node to the HUGR.

Args:
    value: Value of the constant.
    parent: Parent node of added node. Defaults to HUGR root if None.
    metadata: A dictionary of metadata to associate with the node.
        Defaults to None.

Returns:
    Handle to the added node.

Examples:
    >>> h = Hugr()
    >>> n = h.add_const(val.TRUE)
    >>> h[n].op
    Const(TRUE)

methods.name: hugr.hugr.base.Hugr.delete_node
documentation:
Delete a node from the HUGR.

Args:
    node: Node to delete.

Returns:
    The deleted node data, or None if the node was not found.

Examples:
    >>> h = Hugr()
    >>> n = h.add_const(val.TRUE)
    >>> deleted = h.delete_node(n)
    >>> deleted.op
    Const(TRUE)
    >>> len(h)
    1

methods.name: hugr.hugr.base.Hugr.add_link
documentation:
Add a link (edge) between two nodes to the HUGR,
  from an outgoing port to an incoming port.

Args:
    src: Source port.
    dst: Destination port.

Examples:
    >>> df = dfg.Dfg(tys.Bool)
    >>> df.hugr.add_link(df.input_node.out(0), df.output_node.inp(0))
    >>> list(df.hugr.linked_ports(df.input_node[0]))
    [InPort(Node(2), 0)]

methods.name: hugr.hugr.base.Hugr.delete_link
documentation:
Delete a link (edge) between two nodes from the HUGR.

Args:
    src: Source port.
    dst: Destination port.

methods.name: hugr.hugr.base.Hugr.root_op
documentation:
The operation of the root node.

Examples:
    >>> h = Hugr()
    >>> h.root_op()
    Module()

methods.name: hugr.hugr.base.Hugr.num_nodes
documentation:
The number of nodes in the HUGR.

Examples:
    >>> h = Hugr()
    >>> n = h.add_const(val.TRUE)
    >>> h.num_nodes()
    2

methods.name: hugr.hugr.base.Hugr.num_ports
documentation:
The number of ports of a node in a given direction.
Not necessarily the number of connected ports - if port `i` is
connected, then all ports `0..i` are assumed to exist.

Args:
    node: Node to query.
    direction: Direction of ports to count.

Examples:
    >>> h = Hugr()
    >>> n1 = h.add_const(val.TRUE)
    >>> n2 = h.add_const(val.FALSE)
    >>> h.add_link(n1.out(0), n2.inp(2)) # not a valid link!
    >>> h.num_ports(n1, Direction.OUTGOING)
    1
    >>> h.num_ports(n2, Direction.INCOMING)
    3

methods.name: hugr.hugr.base.Hugr.num_in_ports
documentation: The number of incoming ports of a node. See {meth}`num_ports`.

methods.name: hugr.hugr.base.Hugr.num_out_ports
documentation: The number of outgoing ports of a node. See {meth}`num_ports`.

methods.name: hugr.hugr.base.Hugr.linked_ports
documentation:
Return an iterable of In(Out)Ports linked to given Out(In)Port.

Args:
    port: Given port.

Returns:
    Iterator over linked ports.

Examples:
    >>> df = dfg.Dfg(tys.Bool)
    >>> df.set_outputs(df.input_node[0])
    >>> list(df.hugr.linked_ports(df.input_node[0]))
    [InPort(Node(2), 0)]

methods.name: hugr.hugr.base.Hugr.outgoing_order_links
documentation:
Iterator over nodes connected by an outgoing state order link from a
given node.

Args:
    node: Source node of state order link.

Examples:
    >>> df = dfg.Dfg()
    >>> df.add_state_order(df.input_node, df.output_node)
    >>> list(df.hugr.outgoing_order_links(df.input_node))
    [Node(2)]
TODO: single linked port

methods.name: hugr.build.tracked_dfg.TrackedDfg.track_wire
documentation:
Add a wire from this DFG to the tracked wires, and return its index.

Args:

Examples:

    >>> dfg.track_wire(dfg.inputs()[0])

methods.name: hugr.build.tracked_dfg.TrackedDfg.untrack_wire
documentation:
Untrack a wire by index and return it.

Args:

Returns:

Raises:
    IndexError: If the index is not a tracked wire.

Examples:
    >>> dfg = TrackedDfg(tys.Bool, tys.Unit)
    >>> w = dfg.inputs()[0]
    >>> idx = dfg.track_wire(w)
    >>> dfg.untrack_wire(idx) == w

documentation:
Get the tracked wire at the given index.

Args:
    index: Index of the tracked wire.

Raises:
    IndexError: If the index is not a tracked wire.

Returns:
    Tracked wire

Examples:
    >>> dfg = TrackedDfg(tys.Bool, tys.Unit, track_inputs=True)
    >>> dfg.tracked_wire(0) == dfg.inputs()[0]
    True

methods.name: hugr.build.tracked_dfg.TrackedDfg.add
documentation:
Add a command to the DFG.

Overrides {meth}`Dfg.add <hugr.dfg.Dfg.add>` to allow Command inputs
to be either {class}`Wire <hugr.node_port.Wire>` or indices to tracked wires.

Any incoming {class}`Wire <hugr.node_port.Wire>` will
be connected directly, while any integer will be treated as a reference
to the tracked wire at that index.

Any tracked wires will be updated to the output of the new node at the same port
as the incoming index.

Args:

: com: Command to append.
  metadata: Metadata to attach to the function definition. Defaults to None.

Returns:

: The new node.

Raises:

: IndexError: If any input index is not a tracked wire.

Examples:
: ```pycon
  >>> dfg = TrackedDfg(tys.Bool, track_inputs=True)
  >>> dfg.tracked
  [OutPort(Node(1), 0)]
  >>> dfg.add(ops.Noop()(0))
  Node(3)
  >>> dfg.tracked
  [OutPort(Node(3), 0)]
  ```

methods.name: hugr.build.tracked_dfg.TrackedDfg.set_indexed_outputs
documentation:
Set the Dfg outputs, using either {class}`Wire <hugr.node_port.Wire>` or
indices to tracked wires.

Args:

: in_wires: Wires/indices to set as outputs.

Raises:

: IndexError: If any input index is not a tracked wire.

Examples:
: ```pycon
  >>> dfg = TrackedDfg(tys.Bool, tys.Unit)
  >>> (b, i) = dfg.inputs()
  >>> dfg.track_wire(b)
  0
  >>> dfg.set_indexed_outputs(0, i)
  ```

methods.name: hugr.build.tracked_dfg.TrackedDfg.set_tracked_outputs
documentation:
Set the Dfg outputs to the tracked wires.


Examples:
    >>> dfg = TrackedDfg(tys.Bool, tys.Unit, track_inputs=True)
    >>> dfg.set_tracked_outputs()

Dfg builder to append operations to wires by index.

Args:
    *input_types: Input types of the Dfg.
    track_inputs: Whether to track the input wires.

Examples:
    >>> dfg = TrackedDfg(tys.Bool, tys.Unit, track_inputs=True)
    >>> dfg.tracked
    [OutPort(Node(1), 0), OutPort(Node(1), 1)]

documentation: Finish building the 'if' branch and start building the 'else' branch.

Build the 'if' branch of a conditional branching on a boolean value.

Examples:
    >>> from hugr.dfg import Dfg
    >>> dfg = Dfg(tys.Qubit)
    >>> (q,) = dfg.inputs()
    >>> if_ = dfg.add_if(dfg.load(val.TRUE), q)
    >>> if_.set_outputs(if_.input_node[0])
    >>> else_= if_.add_else()
    >>> else_.set_outputs(else_.input_node[0])
    >>> dfg.hugr[else_.conditional_node].op

Convert a python bool to a HUGR boolean value.

Example:
    >>> bool_value(True)
    TRUE
    >>> bool_value(False)
    FALSE

documentation: Convert to serializable model.

methods.name: hugr.val.Value.type_
documentation:
Report the type of the value.

Example:
    >>> TRUE.type_()
    Bool
Abstract value definition. Must be serializable into a HUGR value.

classes.name: hugr.val.Sum
documentation:
Sum-of-product value.

Example:
    >>> Sum(0, tys.Sum([[tys.Bool], [tys.Unit]]), [TRUE])
    Sum(tag=0, typ=Sum([[Bool], [Unit]]), vals=[TRUE])

classes.name: hugr.val.UnitSum
documentation:
Simple {class}`Sum` with each variant being an empty row.

Example:
: ```pycon
  >>> UnitSum(0, 3)
  UnitSum(0, 3)
  >>> UnitSum(0, 1)
  Unit
  >>> assert UnitSum(0, 2) == FALSE
  >>> assert UnitSum(1, 2) == TRUE
  ```

classes.name: hugr.val.Tuple

methods.name: hugr.val.Tuple._to_serial
documentation: sops.TupleValue isn't an instance of sops.SumValue
so mypy doesn't like the override of Sum._to_serial

Tuple or product value, defined by a list of values.
Internally a {class}`Sum` with a single variant row.

Example:
: ```pycon
  >>> tup = Tuple(TRUE, FALSE)
  >>> tup
  Tuple(TRUE, FALSE)
  >>> tup.type_()
  Tuple(Bool, Bool)
  ```

classes.name: hugr.val.Some
documentation:
Optional tuple of value, containing a list of values.

Example:
    >>> some = Some(TRUE, FALSE)
    >>> str(some)
    'Some(TRUE, FALSE)'
    >>> some.type_()
    Option(Bool, Bool)

classes.name: hugr.val.None_
documentation:
Optional tuple of value, containing no values.

Example:
    >>> none = None_(tys.Bool)
    >>> str(none)
    'None'
    >>> none.type_()
    Option(Bool)

classes.name: hugr.val.Left
documentation:
Left variant of a {class}`tys.Either` type, containing a list of values.

In fallible contexts, this represents the failure variant.

Example:
: ```pycon
  >>> left = Left([TRUE, FALSE], [tys.Bool])
  >>> str(left)
  'Left(TRUE, FALSE)'
  >>> str(left.type_())
  'Either((Bool, Bool), Bool)'
  ```

classes.name: hugr.val.Right
documentation:
Right variant of a {class}`tys.Either` type, containing a list of values.

In fallible contexts, this represents the success variant.

Internally a {class}`Sum` with two variant rows.

Example:
: ```pycon
  >>> right = Right([tys.Bool, tys.Bool, tys.Bool], [TRUE, FALSE])
  >>> str(right)
  'Right(TRUE, FALSE)'
  >>> str(right.type_())
  'Either((Bool, Bool, Bool), (Bool, Bool))'
  ```

classes.name: hugr.build.dfg.DefinitionBuilder

methods.name: hugr.build.dfg.DefinitionBuilder.define_function
documentation:
Start building a function definition in the graph.

Args:
    name: The name of the function.
    input_types: The input types for the function.
    output_types: The output types for the function.
        If not provided, it will be inferred after the function is built.
    type_params: The type parameters for the function, if polymorphic.
    parent: The parent node of the constant. Defaults to the root node.

Returns:
    The new function builder.

methods.name: hugr.build.dfg.DefinitionBuilder.add_const
documentation:
Add a static constant to the graph.

Args:

: value: The constant value to add.
  parent: The parent node of the constant. Defaults to the root node.

Returns:

: The node holding the {class}`Const <hugr.ops.Const>` operation.

Example:
: ```pycon
  >>> dfg = Dfg()
  >>> const_n = dfg.add_const(val.TRUE)
  >>> dfg.hugr[const_n].op
  Const(TRUE)
  ```

methods.name: hugr.build.dfg.DefinitionBuilder.add_alias_defn
documentation: Add a type alias definition.

Base class for builders that can define functions, constants, and aliases.

As this class may be a root node, it does not extend `ParentBuilder`.

classes.name: hugr.build.dfg.DfBase

methods.name: hugr.build.dfg.DfBase.new_nested
documentation:
Start building a dataflow graph nested inside a larger HUGR.

Args:
    parent_op: The parent operation of the new dataflow graph.
    hugr: The host HUGR instance to build the dataflow graph in.
    parent: Parent of new dataflow graph's root node: defaults to the
    host HUGR root.

Example:
    >>> hugr = Hugr()
    >>> dfg = Dfg.new_nested(ops.DFG([]), hugr)
    >>> dfg.parent_node
    Node(1)

methods.name: hugr.build.dfg.DfBase.inputs
documentation:
List all incoming wires (output ports of the input node).

Example:
    >>> dfg = Dfg(tys.Bool)
    >>> dfg.inputs()
    [OutPort(Node(1), 0)]

methods.name: hugr.build.dfg.DfBase.add_op
documentation:
Add a dataflow operation to the graph, wiring in input ports.

Args:
    op: The operation to add.
    args: The input wires to the operation.
    metadata: Metadata to attach to the function definition. Defaults to None.

Returns:
    The node holding the new operation.

Example:
    >>> dfg = Dfg(tys.Bool)
    >>> dfg.add_op(ops.Noop(), dfg.inputs()[0])
    Node(3)

methods.name: hugr.build.dfg.DfBase.add
documentation:
Add a command (holding a dataflow operation and the incoming wires)
to the graph.

Args:
    com: The command to add.
    metadata: Metadata to attach to the function definition. Defaults to None.

Example:
    >>> dfg = Dfg(tys.Bool)
    >>> (i,) = dfg.inputs()
    >>> dfg.add(ops.Noop()(i))
    Node(3)

methods.name: hugr.build.dfg.DfBase.extend
documentation:
Add a series of commands to the DFG.

Shorthand for calling {meth}`add` on each command in `coms`.

Args:

: coms: Commands to add.

Returns:

: List of the new nodes in the same order as the commands.

Raises:

: IndexError: If any input index is not a tracked wire.

Examples:
: ```pycon
  >>> dfg = Dfg(tys.Bool, tys.Unit)
  >>> (b, u) = dfg.inputs()
  >>> dfg.extend(ops.Noop()(b), ops.Noop()(u))
  [Node(3), Node(4)]
  ```

methods.name: hugr.build.dfg.DfBase.insert_nested
documentation:
Insert a nested dataflow graph into the current graph, wiring in the
inputs.

Args:
    dfg: The dataflow graph to insert.
    args: The input wires to the graph.

Returns:
    The root node of the inserted graph.

Example:
    >>> dfg = Dfg(tys.Bool)
    >>> dfg2 = Dfg(tys.Bool)
    >>> dfg.insert_nested(dfg2, dfg.inputs()[0])
    Node(3)

methods.name: hugr.build.dfg.DfBase.add_nested
documentation:
Start building a nested dataflow graph.

Args:
    args: The input wires to the nested DFG.

Returns:
    Builder for new nested dataflow graph.

Example:
    >>> dfg = Dfg(tys.Bool)
    >>> with dfg.add_nested(dfg.inputs()[0]) as dfg2:                   dfg2.parent_node
    Node(3)

methods.name: hugr.build.dfg.DfBase.add_cfg
documentation:
Start building a new CFG nested inside the current dataflow graph.

Args:
    args: The input wires to the new CFG.

Returns:
    Builder for new nested CFG.

Example:
    >>> dfg = Dfg(tys.Bool)
    >>> with dfg.add_cfg(dfg.inputs()[0]) as cfg:                    cfg.parent_op
    CFG(inputs=[Bool])

methods.name: hugr.build.dfg.DfBase.insert_cfg
documentation:
Insert a CFG into the current dataflow graph, wiring in the inputs.

Args:
    cfg: The CFG to insert.
    args: The input wires to the CFG.

Returns:
    The root node of the inserted CFG.

Example:
    >>> from hugr.cfg import Cfg
    >>> dfg = Dfg(tys.Bool)
    >>> cfg = Cfg(tys.Bool)
    >>> dfg.insert_cfg(cfg, dfg.inputs()[0])
    Node(3)

methods.name: hugr.build.dfg.DfBase.add_conditional
documentation:
Start building a new conditional nested inside the current dataflow
graph.

Args:
    cond_wire: The wire holding the value (of Sum type) to branch the
    conditional on.
    args: Remaining input wires to the conditional.

Returns:
    Builder for new nested conditional.

Example:
    >>> dfg = Dfg(tys.Bool, tys.Unit)
    >>> (cond, unit) = dfg.inputs()
    >>> cond = dfg.add_conditional(cond, unit)
    >>> cond.parent_node
    Node(3)

methods.name: hugr.build.dfg.DfBase.insert_conditional
documentation:
Insert a conditional into the current dataflow graph, wiring in the
inputs.

Args:
    cond: The conditional to insert.
    cond_wire: The wire holding the value (of Sum type)
      to branch the Conditional on.
    args: Remaining input wires to the conditional.

Returns:
    The root node of the inserted conditional.

Example:
    >>> from hugr.cond_loop import Conditional
    >>> cond = Conditional(tys.Bool, [])
    >>> dfg = Dfg(tys.Bool)
    >>> cond_n = dfg.insert_conditional(cond, dfg.inputs()[0])
    >>> dfg.hugr[cond_n].op
    Conditional(sum_ty=Bool, other_inputs=[])

methods.name: hugr.build.dfg.DfBase.add_if
documentation:
Start building a new if block nested inside the current dataflow
graph.

Args:
    cond_wire: The wire holding the Bool value to branch the If on.
    args: Remaining input wires to the If (and subsequent Else).

Returns:
    Builder for new nested If.

Example:
    >>> dfg = Dfg(tys.Bool)
    >>> (cond,) = dfg.inputs()
    >>> if_ = dfg.add_if(cond, cond)
    >>> if_.parent_op
    Case(inputs=[Bool])

methods.name: hugr.build.dfg.DfBase.add_tail_loop
documentation:
Start building a new tail loop nested inside the current dataflow
graph.

Args:
    just_inputs: input wires for types that are only inputs to the loop body.
    rest: input wires for types that are inputs and outputs of the loop
    body.

Returns:
    Builder for new nested TailLoop.

Example:
    >>> dfg = Dfg(tys.Bool)
    >>> (cond,) = dfg.inputs()
    >>> tl = dfg.add_tail_loop([cond], [cond])
    >>> tl.parent_op
    TailLoop(just_inputs=[Bool], rest=[Bool])

methods.name: hugr.build.dfg.DfBase.insert_tail_loop
documentation:
Insert a tail loop into the current dataflow graph, wiring in the
inputs.

Args:
    tl: The tail loop to insert.
    just_inputs: input wires for types that are only inputs to the loop body.
    rest: input wires for types that are inputs and outputs of the loop
    body.

Returns:
    The root node of the inserted tail loop.

Example:
    >>> from hugr.cond_loop import TailLoop
    >>> tl = TailLoop([tys.Bool], [tys.Bool])
    >>> dfg = Dfg(tys.Bool)
    >>> (b,) = dfg.inputs()
    >>> tl_n = dfg.insert_tail_loop(tl, [b], [b])
    >>> dfg.hugr[tl_n].op
    TailLoop(just_inputs=[Bool], rest=[Bool])

methods.name: hugr.build.dfg.DfBase.set_outputs
documentation:
Set the outputs of the dataflow graph.
Connects wires to the output node.

Args:
    args: Wires to connect to the output node.

Example:
    >>> dfg = Dfg(tys.Bool)
    >>> dfg.set_outputs(dfg.inputs()[0]) # connect input to output

methods.name: hugr.build.dfg.DfBase._set_parent_output_count
documentation:
Set the final number of output ports on the parent operation.

Args:
    count: The number of output ports.

Example:
    >>> dfg = Dfg(tys.Bool)
    >>> dfg._set_parent_output_count(2)

methods.name: hugr.build.dfg.DfBase.add_state_order
documentation:
Add a state order link between two nodes.

Args:
    src: The source node.
    dst: The destination node.

Examples:
    >>> df = dfg.Dfg()
    >>> df.add_state_order(df.input_node, df.output_node)
    >>> list(df.hugr.outgoing_order_links(df.input_node))
    [Node(2)]

methods.name: hugr.build.dfg.DfBase.load
documentation:
Load a constant into the graph as a dataflow value.

Args:
: const: The constant to load, either a Value that will be added as a

  : child Const node then loaded, or a node corresponding to an
    existing Const.

  const_parent: If `const` is a Value, the parent node for the new

  : constant definition. Defaults to the current dataflow container.

Returns:

: The node holding the {class}`LoadConst <hugr.ops.LoadConst>`
  operation.

Example:
: ```pycon
  >>> dfg = Dfg()
  >>> const_n = dfg.load(val.TRUE)
  >>> len(dfg.hugr) # parent, input, output, const, load
  5
  >>> dfg.hugr[const_n].op
  LoadConst(Bool)
  ```

methods.name: hugr.build.dfg.DfBase.call
documentation:
Call a static function in the graph.
See {class}`Call <hugr.ops.Call>` for more on how polymorphic functions
are handled.

Args:

: func: The node corresponding to the function definition/declaration to call.
  args: The input wires to the function call.
  instantiation: The concrete function type to call (needed if polymorphic).
  type_args: The type arguments for the function (needed if
  polymorphic).

Returns:

: The node holding the {class}`Call <hugr.ops.Call>` operation.

methods.name: hugr.build.dfg.DfBase.load_function
documentation:
Load a static function into the graph as a higher-order value.

Args:

: func: The node corresponding to the function definition/declaration to load.
  instantiation: The concrete function type to load (needed if polymorphic).
  type_args: The type arguments for the function (needed if
  polymorphic).

Returns:

: The node holding the {class}`LoadFunc <hugr.ops.LoadFunc>` operation.

Base class for dataflow graph builders.

Args:
    parent_op: The parent operation of the dataflow graph.

classes.name: hugr.build.dfg.Dfg
documentation:
Builder for a simple nested Dataflow graph, with root node of type
{class}`DFG <hugr.ops.DFG>`.

Args:

: input_types: The input types of the the dataflow graph. Output types are
  calculated by propagating types through the graph.

Example:
: ```pycon
  >>> dfg = Dfg(tys.Bool)
  >>> dfg.parent_op
  DFG(inputs=[Bool])
  ```

classes.name: hugr.build.dfg.Function

methods.name: hugr.build.dfg.Function.declare_outputs
documentation:
Declare the output types of the function.

This is required when calling a function which hasn't been completely
defined yet. The wires passed to {meth}`set_outputs` must match the
declared output types.

methods.name: hugr.build.dfg.Function.set_outputs
documentation:
Set the outputs of the dataflow graph.
Connects wires to the output node.

If {meth}`declare_outputs` has been called, the wire types must match
the declared output types.

Args:

: args: Wires to connect to the output node.

Example:
: ```pycon
  >>> dfg = Dfg(tys.Bool)
  >>> dfg.set_outputs(dfg.inputs()[0]) # connect input to output
  ```

Build a function definition as a HUGR dataflow graph.

Args:
    name: The name of the function.
    input_types: The input types for the function (output types are
    computed by propagating types from input node through the graph).
    type_params: The type parameters for the function, if polymorphic.

Examples:
    >>> f = Function("f", [tys.Bool])
    >>> f.parent_op
    FuncDefn(f_name='f', inputs=[Bool], params=[])

functions.name: hugr.tys.get_first_sum
documentation:
Check the first type in a row of types is a {class}`Sum`, returning it
and the rest.

Args:

: types: row of types.

Raises:

: AssertionError: if the first type is not a {class}`Sum`.

Example:
: ```pycon
  >>> get_first_sum([UnitSum(3), Qubit])
  (UnitSum(3), [Qubit])
  >>> get_first_sum([Qubit, UnitSum(3)]) # doctest: +IGNORE_EXCEPTION_DETAIL
  Traceback (most recent call last):
  AssertionError: Expected Sum, got Qubit
  ```

methods.name: hugr.tys.Sum.resolve
documentation: Resolve types in the sum type using the given registry.

Algebraic sum-over-product type. Instances of this type correspond to
tuples (products) over one of the `variant_rows` in the sum type, tagged by
the index of the row.

classes.name: hugr.tys.UnitSum
documentation: Simple {class}`Sum` type with `size` variants of empty rows.

classes.name: hugr.tys.Tuple
documentation:
Product type with `tys` elements. Instances of this type correspond to
{class}`Sum` with a single variant.

classes.name: hugr.tys.Option
documentation:
Optional tuple of elements.

Instances of this type correspond to {class}`Sum` with two variants.
The second variant is the tuple of elements, the first is empty.

classes.name: hugr.tys.Either
documentation:
Two-variant tuple of elements.

Instances of this type correspond to {class}`Sum` with a Left and a Right variant.

In fallible contexts, the Right variant is used to represent success, and the
Left variant is used to represent failure.

classes.name: hugr.tys.FunctionType

methods.name: hugr.tys.FunctionType.empty
documentation:
Generate an empty function type.

Example:
    >>> FunctionType.empty()
    FunctionType([], [])

methods.name: hugr.tys.FunctionType.endo
documentation:
Function type with the same input and output types.

Example:
    >>> FunctionType.endo([Qubit])
    FunctionType([Qubit], [Qubit])

methods.name: hugr.tys.FunctionType.flip
documentation:
Return a new function type with input and output types swapped.

Example:
    >>> FunctionType([Qubit], [Bool]).flip()
    FunctionType([Bool], [Qubit])

methods.name: hugr.tys.FunctionType.resolve
documentation: Resolve types in the function type using the given registry.

A function type, defined by input types,
output types and extension requirements.

classes.name: hugr.tys.PolyFuncType

methods.name: hugr.tys.PolyFuncType.resolve
documentation: Resolve types in the polymorphic function type using the given registry.

methods.name: hugr.tys.PolyFuncType.empty
documentation:
Generate an empty polymorphic function type.

Example:
    >>> PolyFuncType.empty()
    PolyFuncType(params=[], body=FunctionType([], []))

Polymorphic function type or type scheme. Defined by a list of type
parameters that may appear in the {class}`FunctionType` body.

classes.name: hugr.tys.ExtType
documentation: Extension type, defined by a type definition and type arguments.

classes.name: hugr.tys.Opaque

methods.name: hugr.tys.Opaque.resolve
documentation:
Resolve the opaque type to an {class}`ExtType` using the given registry.

If the extension or type is not found, return the original type.
Opaque type, identified by `id` and with optional type arguments and bound.

modules.name: hugr.ops
documentation: Definitions of HUGR operations.

classes.name: hugr.ops.InvalidPort
documentation: Port is not valid for this operation.

classes.name: hugr.ops.Op

methods.name: hugr.ops.Op.num_out
documentation:
The number of output ports for this operation.

Example:
    >>> op = Const(val.TRUE)
    >>> op.num_out
    1

methods.name: hugr.ops.Op._to_serial
documentation: Convert this operation to a serializable form.

methods.name: hugr.ops.Op.port_kind
documentation:
Get the kind of the given port.

Example:
    >>> op = Const(val.TRUE)
    >>> op.port_kind(OutPort(Node(0), 0))
    ConstKind(Bool)

methods.name: hugr.ops.Op.name
documentation: Name of the operation.

An abstract HUGR operation. Must be convertible
to a serializable {class}`BaseOp`.

classes.name: hugr.ops.DataflowOp

methods.name: hugr.ops.DataflowOp.outer_signature
documentation:
The external signature of this operation. Defines the valid external
connectivity of the node the operation belongs to.

methods.name: hugr.ops.DataflowOp.port_type
documentation:
Get the type of the given dataflow port from the signature of the
operation.

Example:
    >>> op = Input([tys.Bool])
    >>> op.port_type(OutPort(Node(0), 0))
    Bool

methods.name: hugr.ops.DataflowOp.__call__
documentation:
Calling with incoming {class}`Wire` arguments returns a
{class}`Command` which can be used to wire the operation into a
dataflow graph.

Abstract dataflow operation. Can be assumed to have a signature and Value-
kind ports.

classes.name: hugr.ops.IncompleteOp
documentation: Op types have not been set during building.

classes.name: hugr.ops.Command
documentation:
A {class}`DataflowOp` and its incoming {class}`Wire <hugr.nodeport.Wire>`

: arguments.

Ephemeral: used to wire operations into a dataflow graph.

Example:
: ```pycon
  >>> Noop()(Node(0).out(0))
  Command(op=Noop, incoming=[OutPort(Node(0), 0)])
  ```

classes.name: hugr.ops.Input
documentation:
Input operation in dataflow graph. Outputs of this operation are the
inputs to the graph.

classes.name: hugr.ops.Output
documentation:
Output operation in dataflow graph. Inputs of this operation are the
outputs of the graph.

classes.name: hugr.ops.AsExtOp

methods.name: hugr.ops.AsExtOp.ext_op
documentation:
{class}`ExtOp` operation that this type represents.

Computed once using {meth}`op_def` {meth}`type_args` and {meth}`type_args`.
Each of those methods should be deterministic.

methods.name: hugr.ops.AsExtOp.op_def
documentation:
The {class}`tys.OpDef` for this operation.

Used by {attr}`ext_op`, so must be deterministic.

methods.name: hugr.ops.AsExtOp.type_args
documentation:
Type arguments of the operation.

Used by {attr}`op_def`, so must be deterministic.

methods.name: hugr.ops.AsExtOp.cached_signature
documentation:
Cached signature of the operation, if there is one.

Used by {attr}`op_def`, so must be deterministic.

methods.name: hugr.ops.AsExtOp.from_ext
documentation:
Load from a {class}`ExtOp` operation.

By default assumes the type of `cls` is a singleton,
and compares the result of {meth}`to_ext` with the given `ext_op`.

If successful, returns the singleton, else None.

Non-singleton types should override this method.

Raises:

: InvalidCustomOp: If the given `ext_op` does not match the expected one for a
  given extension/operation name.

inner_classes.name: hugr.ops.AsExtOp.InvalidExtOp
documentation: Extension operation does not match the expected type.

Abstract interface that types can implement
to behave as an extension dataflow operation.

classes.name: hugr.ops.Custom

methods.name: hugr.ops.Custom.check_id
documentation: Check if the operation matches the given extension and operation name.

methods.name: hugr.ops.Custom.resolve
documentation:
Resolve the custom operation to an {class}`ExtOp`.

If extension or operation is not found, returns itself.
Serializable version of non-core dataflow operation defined in an extension.

classes.name: hugr.ops.ExtOp
documentation: A non-core dataflow operation defined in an extension.

classes.name: hugr.ops.RegisteredOp

class_variables.name: hugr.ops.RegisteredOp.const_op_def
documentation: may be set by registered_op decorator.

Base class for operations that are registered with an extension using
{meth}`Extension.register_op <hugr.ext.Extension.register_op>`.

classes.name: hugr.ops.MakeTuple

methods.name: hugr.ops.MakeTuple.types
documentation:
If set, the types of the tuple elements.

Raises:
    IncompleteOp: If the types have not been set.
Operation to create a tuple from a sequence of wires.

classes.name: hugr.ops.UnpackTuple

methods.name: hugr.ops.UnpackTuple.types
documentation:
If set, the types of the tuple elements.

Raises:
    IncompleteOp: If the types have not been set.
Operation to unpack a tuple into its elements.

classes.name: hugr.ops.Tag
documentation:
Tag a row of incoming values to make them a variant of a sum type.

Requires `sum_ty` to be set as it is not possible to extract all the variants from
just the input wires for one variant.

classes.name: hugr.ops.DfParentOp

methods.name: hugr.ops.DfParentOp.inner_signature
documentation: Inner signature of the child dataflow graph.

Abstract parent of dataflow graph operations. Can be queried for the
dataflow signature of its child graph.

classes.name: hugr.ops.DFG

methods.name: hugr.ops.DFG.outputs
documentation:
Output types of the operation.

Raises:
    IncompleteOp: If the outputs have not been set.

methods.name: hugr.ops.DFG.signature
documentation:
Signature of the operation.

Raises:
    IncompleteOp: If the outputs have not been set.
Simple dataflow graph operation. Outer signature matches inner signature.

classes.name: hugr.ops.CFG

methods.name: hugr.ops.CFG.outputs
documentation:
Output types of the operation, if set.

Raises:
    IncompleteOp: If the outputs have not been set.

methods.name: hugr.ops.CFG.signature
documentation:
Dataflow signature of the CFG operation.

Raises:
    IncompleteOp: If the outputs have not been set.

classes.name: hugr.ops.DataflowBlock

methods.name: hugr.ops.DataflowBlock.sum_ty
documentation:
If set, the sum type that defines the potential branching of the
block.


Raises:
    IncompleteOp: If the sum type has not been set.

methods.name: hugr.ops.DataflowBlock.other_outputs
documentation:
The non-branching outputs of the block which are passed to all
successors.

Raises:
    IncompleteOp: If the outputs have not been set.

methods.name: hugr.ops.DataflowBlock.nth_outputs
documentation:
The outputs passed to the nth successor of the block.
Concatenation of the nth variant of the sum type and the other outputs.
Parent of non-entry basic block in a control flow graph.

classes.name: hugr.ops.ExitBlock

methods.name: hugr.ops.ExitBlock.cfg_outputs
documentation:
Output types of the parent control flow graph of this exit block.

Raises:
    IncompleteOp: If the outputs have not been set.
Unique exit block of a control flow graph.

classes.name: hugr.ops.Const
documentation:
A static constant value. Can be used with a {class}`LoadConst` to load into
a dataflow graph.

classes.name: hugr.ops.LoadConst

methods.name: hugr.ops.LoadConst.type_
documentation:
The type of the loaded value.

Raises:
    IncompleteOp: If the type has not been set.
Load a constant value into a dataflow graph. Connects to a {class}`Const`.

classes.name: hugr.ops.Conditional

methods.name: hugr.ops.Conditional.outputs
documentation:
Outputs of the conditional, common to all cases.

Raises:
    IncompleteOp: If the outputs have not been set.

methods.name: hugr.ops.Conditional.signature
documentation:
Dataflow signature of the conditional operation.

Raises:
    IncompleteOp: If the outputs have not been set.

methods.name: hugr.ops.Conditional.nth_inputs
documentation:
The inputs passed to the nth child case.
Concatenation of the nth variant of the sum type and the other inputs.

'Switch' operation on the variants of an incoming sum type, evaluating the
corresponding one of the child {class}`Case` operations.

classes.name: hugr.ops.Case

methods.name: hugr.ops.Case.outputs
documentation:
Outputs of the case operation.

Raises:
    IncompleteOp: If the outputs have not been set.
Parent of a dataflow graph that is a branch of a {class}`Conditional`.

classes.name: hugr.ops.TailLoop

methods.name: hugr.ops.TailLoop.just_outputs
documentation:
Types that are only outputs of the child graph.

Raises:
    IncompleteOp: If the outputs have not been set.

Tail controlled loop operation, child dataflow graph iterates while it
outputs the first variant of a sum type.

classes.name: hugr.ops.FuncDefn

methods.name: hugr.ops.FuncDefn.outputs

Output types of the function.

Raises:
    IncompleteOp: If the outputs have not been set.

methods.name: hugr.ops.FuncDefn.signature
documentation:
Polymorphic signature of the function.

Raises:
    IncompleteOp: If the outputs have not been set.

Function definition operation, parent of a dataflow graph that defines

classes.name: hugr.ops.FuncDecl
documentation: Function declaration operation, defines the signature of a function.

classes.name: hugr.ops.Module
documentation: Root operation of a HUGR which corresponds to a full module definition.

classes.name: hugr.ops.NoConcreteFunc
documentation: Could not instantiate a polymorphic function.

classes.name: hugr.ops.Call
documentation:
Call a function inside a dataflow graph. Connects to {class}`FuncDefn` or
{class}`FuncDecl` operations.

Args:

: signature: Polymorphic function signature.
  instantiation: Concrete function signature. Defaults to None.
  type_args: Type arguments for polymorphic function. Defaults to None.

Raises:
: NoConcreteFunc: If the signature is polymorphic and no instantiation

  : is provided.

classes.name: hugr.ops.CallIndirect

methods.name: hugr.ops.CallIndirect.signature
documentation:
The signature of the function being called.

Raises:
IncompleteOp: If the signature has not been set.

Higher order evaluation of a
{class}`FunctionType <hugr.tys.FunctionType>` value.

classes.name: hugr.ops.LoadFunc
documentation:
Load a statically defined function as a higher order value.

: Connects to {class}`FuncDefn` or {class}`FuncDecl` operations.

Args:

: signature: Polymorphic function signature.
  instantiation: Concrete function signature. Defaults to None.
  type_args: Type arguments for polymorphic function. Defaults to None.

Raises:
: NoConcreteFunc: If the signature is polymorphic and no instantiation

  : is provided.

methods.name: hugr.ops.Noop.type_
